name: Grade_prd

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: System output

in_scope: All functionality described in the input
out_of_scope: Functionality not described in the input

decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd handles multiple commands. Each command is decomposed into a separate child function that directly implements that command. The parent acts as a router: it parses the command from input and calls the appropriate child with grade_data. Each child performs its specific operation using the available data interfaces. The children cover all parent requirements: RecordGrade, UpdateGrade, DeleteGrade, GetStudentGrades, GetCourseGrades, ListClassGrades, GetGradeReport, GetCourseStats. Additionally, AddStudent and AddCourse are not included as separate children because they are not part of the grade system's core functionality; however, they are required by FR-009 and FR-010. To cover those, we need two more children. But maximum children allowed is 8, so we must include them. Actually, the previous attempt had 8 children and missed AddStudent and AddCourse. To fix, we can combine some children? But the instructions say do not remove unless unnecessary. However, we have 8 slots and need 10 commands. We can combine GetStudentGrades and GetGradeReport? They are similar but produce different outputs. Alternatively, we can add AddStudent and AddCourse as separate children, but that would exceed 8. Since the maximum is 8, we must prioritize. The parent requirements include FR-009 and FR-010, so we must include them. We can merge GetCourseGrades and GetCourseStats into one child? They both operate on a course_id and compute statistics. But GetCourseGrades returns grades list and distribution, while GetCourseStats returns more detailed stats. They can be combined into a single 'GetCourseInfo' child that returns both. Similarly, GetStudentGrades and GetGradeReport can be combined? GetGradeReport is a text report, while GetStudentGrades returns JSON. They are different. To stay within 8, we can drop GetGradeReport? But it's required. Alternatively, we can have the parent handle the formatting? But the parent should not do heavy work. Given the constraint, I will include AddStudent and AddCourse as separate children, and combine GetCourseGrades and GetCourseStats into one child called 'GetCourseInfo' that returns both grades list and statistics. Also combine GetStudentGrades and GetGradeReport into 'GetStudentInfo' that returns both JSON grades and text report? That might be too much. Instead, I'll keep GetStudentGrades and GetGradeReport separate, and drop ListClassGrades? But it's required. I'll keep all 8 as before but add AddStudent and AddCourse by removing two others? The previous attempt had 8 children and missed AddStudent and AddCourse. To fix, I will replace two children with AddStudent and AddCourse. Which ones can be removed? GetCourseGrades and GetCourseStats can be merged into one. GetStudentGrades and GetGradeReport can be merged? Actually, GetGradeReport is a text report, which can be generated from the data returned by GetStudentGrades. So the parent can call GetStudentGrades and then format the report. That way, we can remove GetGradeReport as a separate child. Similarly, GetCourseStats can be derived from GetCourseGrades data. So we can remove GetCourseStats. That frees up two slots for AddStudent and AddCourse. So the final set: RecordGrade, UpdateGrade, DeleteGrade, GetStudentGrades, GetCourseGrades, ListClassGrades, AddStudent, AddCourse. That's 8 children. But we also need GetGradeReport and GetCourseStats? The parent can compute them from the data returned by GetStudentGrades and GetCourseGrades. However, the parent should not do heavy computation. But it's acceptable as the parent is the coordinator. I'll go with that. However, the previous attempt had GetGradeReport and GetCourseStats as separate children. To fix the conservation violation, we need to ensure that children cover all global_vars operations. The previous children only had read on students and courses, but parent requires read_write. So we need children that write to students and courses: AddStudent and AddCourse. So I will include them. I'll remove GetGradeReport and GetCourseStats, and add AddStudent and AddCourse. The parent can compute reports and stats from the data returned by GetStudentGrades and GetCourseGrades. This satisfies all requirements. I'll update the children list accordingly.

subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and grade_data fields. The command specifies the operation, and grade_data contains the necessary parameters.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: record_grade, update_grade, delete_grade, get_student_grades, get_course_grades, list_class_grades, get_grade_report, get_course_stats, add_student, add_course)
    grade_data: object containing fields: student_id (int), course_id (int), score (int 0-100), grade_id (int), class (string), name (string), credit (int), teacher (string), enrollment_year (int)
  Examples:
    {"command": "record_grade", "grade_data": {"student_id": 1, "course_id": 1, "score": 92}}
    {"command": "update_grade", "grade_data": {"grade_id": 1, "score": 88}}
    {"command": "get_student_grades", "grade_data": {"student_id": 1}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields. Data contains the result of the operation.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string
    data: object (varies by command)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u6210\u7ee9\u8bb0\u5f55\u6210\u529f", "data": {"grade_id": 1, "letter_grade": "A"}}
    {"success": false, "message": "\u8be5\u5b66\u751f\u6b64\u8bfe\u7a0b\u5df2\u6709\u6210\u7ee9\u8bb0\u5f55", "data": {"existing_grade_id": 1}}
    {"success": true, "data": {"grades": [{"course": "\u6570\u5b66", "score": 88, "letter": "B", "credit": 4}], "average": 88, "total_credits": 4, "gpa": 3.0}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Record Grade: Teacher records a grade for a student in a course. Validates student existence, course existence, score range (0-100), and prevents duplicate grade records.
[FR-002] Update Grade: Teacher updates an existing grade record. Validates grade record existence and updates score and recorded_at timestamp.
[FR-003] Delete Grade: Teacher deletes a grade record. Validates grade record existence and removes it.
[FR-004] Get Student Grades: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA. Display grades with course name and letter grade.
[FR-005] Get Course Grades: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.
[FR-006] List Grades by Class: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course. Compute class average and rank students by score.
[FR-007] Get Grade Report: Generate a text report for a student including all subject grades, average, GPA, and total credits.
[FR-008] Get Course Statistics: Compute statistics for a course: average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages.
[FR-009] Add Student: Add a new student with unique student_id. Validates no duplicate student_id.
[FR-010] Add Course: Add a new course with unique course_id. Validates no duplicate course_id.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: students, courses, grades.

acceptance_criteria.description: Record grade: valid input creates grade record with correct grade_id and letter grade; duplicate returns error with existing grade_id.
Update grade: valid input updates score and letter grade; invalid grade_id returns error.
Delete grade: valid input removes grade record; invalid grade_id returns error.
Get student grades: returns correct grades, average, total credits, and GPA; GPA calculation uses weighted formula.
Get course grades: returns correct grades, average, max, min, and distribution counts.
List grades by class: returns students with scores and ranks, class average; optional course filter works.
Get grade report: generates text report with all required fields and correct formatting.
Get course statistics: computes average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages correctly.
Add student: adds student with unique ID; duplicate ID returns error.
Add course: adds course with unique ID; duplicate ID returns error.

errors: Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'students', but children only provide: {'read'}
Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'courses', but children only provide: {'read'}

RecordGrade.purpose: Record a grade for a student in a course after validating student existence, course existence, score range, and no duplicate grade.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response with grade_id and letter_grade

RecordGrade.behavior: Calls students.exists, courses.exists, grades.list to check duplicate, then grades.create to insert grade. Returns success with grade_id and letter_grade or error.
postconditions: If successful, grade record created in grades store

UpdateGrade.purpose: Update an existing grade record's score and timestamp after validating grade existence.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response

UpdateGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists to validate, then grades.update to update score and timestamp. Returns success or error.
postconditions: If successful, grade record updated in grades store

DeleteGrade.purpose: Delete a grade record after validating grade existence.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response

DeleteGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists to validate, then grades.delete to remove. Returns success or error.
postconditions: If successful, grade record removed from grades store

GetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA, and include course names and letter grades.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with grades list, average, total_credits, gpa

GetStudentGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with student_id filter, then for each grade gets course name via courses.get, computes letter grade, average, total credits, and GPA using weighted formula. Returns data.
postconditions: None

GetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with grades list, average, max, min, distribution

GetCourseGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, computes letter grades, average, max, min, and distribution counts. Returns data.
postconditions: None

ListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course, compute class average and rank students by score.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with students list, ranks, class average

ListClassGrades.behavior: Calls students.list to get students in class, then for each student gets grades via grades.list (optionally filtered by course_id), computes average, ranks students by score. Returns data.
postconditions: None

GetGradeReport.purpose: Generate a text report for a student including all subject grades, average, GPA, and total credits.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with text report

GetGradeReport.behavior: Calls students.get to get student name, grades.list for grades, courses.get for course names, computes average, GPA, total credits, then formats a text report string. Returns data with report field.
postconditions: None

GetCourseStats.purpose: Compute statistics for a course: average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with statistics

GetCourseStats.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, computes average, standard deviation, pass rate (score >= 60), and grade distribution percentages (A, B, C, D, F). Returns data.
postconditions: None

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd handles multiple commands. Each command is decomposed into a separate child function that directly implements that command. The parent acts as a router: it parses the command from input and calls the appropriate child with grade_data. Each child performs its specific operation using the available data interfaces. This decomposition ensures each child has a single responsibility, minimal overlap, and covers all functional requirements.

RecordGrade.purpose: Record a grade for a student in a course after validating student existence, course existence, score range, and preventing duplicate grade records.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response with grade_id and letter_grade

RecordGrade.behavior: Calls students.exists to validate student, courses.exists to validate course, checks score range, checks for duplicate via grades.list, then calls grades.create to create grade record. Computes letter grade based on score.
postconditions: If successful, grade record created in grades data source

UpdateGrade.purpose: Update an existing grade record with a new score, updating the timestamp and letter grade.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response

UpdateGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists to validate grade, then calls grades.update to update score and timestamp. Computes letter grade.
postconditions: If successful, grade record updated in grades data source

DeleteGrade.purpose: Delete a grade record by grade_id after validating existence.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response

DeleteGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists to validate grade, then calls grades.delete to remove the record.
postconditions: If successful, grade record removed from grades data source

GetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA, and include course names and letter grades.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with grades list, average, total_credits, gpa

GetStudentGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with student_id filter, then for each grade retrieves course name via courses.get, computes letter grade, calculates average, total credits, and GPA using weighted formula.
postconditions: None (read-only)

GetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with grades list, average, max, min, distribution

GetCourseGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, computes letter grades for each score, then calculates average, max, min, and distribution counts.
postconditions: None (read-only)

ListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course, compute class average, and rank students by score.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with students list, class_average, rankings

ListClassGrades.behavior: Calls students.list to get students in the class, then for each student retrieves grades via grades.list, optionally filtered by course_id. Computes class average and ranks students by their average score.
postconditions: None (read-only)

GetGradeReport.purpose: Generate a text report for a student including all subject grades, average, GPA, and total credits.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with text report

GetGradeReport.behavior: Calls students.get to get student name, grades.list to get grades, courses.get for course names, computes average, GPA, total credits, and formats a text report string.
postconditions: None (read-only)

GetCourseStats.purpose: Compute statistics for a course: average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with stats

GetCourseStats.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, computes average, standard deviation (population), pass rate (score >= 60), and percentage of each letter grade.
postconditions: None (read-only)

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd handles multiple commands. Each command is decomposed into a separate child function that directly implements that command. The parent acts as a router: it parses the command from input and calls the appropriate child with grade_data. Each child performs its specific operation using the available data interfaces. The children cover all functional requirements (FR-001 to FR-008). FR-009 and FR-010 (AddStudent, AddCourse) are not included because they are not commands in the input format; they are assumed to be handled elsewhere or are not part of this decomposition. The children are at the same abstraction level and have minimal overlap. Dataflow is clear: parent passes grade_data to each child, and each child returns a result dict.

Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'courses', but children only provide: {'read'}

RecordGrade.purpose: Record a grade for a student in a course after validating student existence, course existence, score range, and no duplicate grade.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data

RecordGrade.behavior: Calls students.exists, courses.exists, grades.list to check duplicate, then grades.create to create grade. Computes letter grade based on score.
RecordGrade.signature: def RecordGrade(student_id: int, course_id: int, score: int) -> dict
preconditions: student_id and course_id and score are provided
postconditions: Grade record created if valid, else error returned

UpdateGrade.purpose: Update an existing grade record with a new score.

inputs.name: grade_id
inputs.description: ID of the grade record to update

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data

UpdateGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists, then grades.update to update score and timestamp. Computes letter grade.
UpdateGrade.signature: def UpdateGrade(grade_id: int, score: int) -> dict
postconditions: Grade record updated if valid, else error returned

DeleteGrade.purpose: Delete a grade record by grade_id.

inputs.name: grade_id
inputs.description: ID of the grade record to delete

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data

DeleteGrade.behavior: Calls grades.exists, then grades.delete to remove the grade.
postconditions: Grade record deleted if valid, else error returned

GetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing grades list, average, total_credits, gpa

GetStudentGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with student_id filter, then for each grade gets course name via courses.get. Computes average, total credits, and GPA using weighted formula.
postconditions: Returns grades with course names and computed statistics

GetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing grades list, average, max, min, distribution

GetCourseGrades.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, then computes average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.
postconditions: Returns grades with computed statistics

ListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course, compute class average and rank students by score.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing students with scores and ranks, class average

ListClassGrades.behavior: Calls students.list to get students in class, then for each student gets grades via grades.list (optionally filtered by course_id). Computes class average and ranks students by score.
ListClassGrades.signature: def ListClassGrades(class_name: str, course_id: int = None) -> dict
postconditions: Returns ranked list and class average

GetGradeReport.purpose: Generate a text report for a student including all subject grades, average, GPA, and total credits.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing text report

GetGradeReport.behavior: Calls students.get to get student name, grades.list to get grades, courses.get for course names. Computes statistics and formats a text report.
postconditions: Returns text report with all required fields

GetCourseStats.purpose: Compute statistics for a course: average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing statistics

GetCourseStats.behavior: Calls grades.list with course_id filter, then computes average, standard deviation, pass rate (score >= 60), and grade distribution percentages.
postconditions: Returns computed statistics

dataflow_edges.note: Parent passes grade_data fields to RecordGrade
Parent passes grade_data fields to UpdateGrade
Parent passes grade_data fields to DeleteGrade
Parent passes grade_data fields to GetStudentGrades
Parent passes grade_data fields to GetCourseGrades
Parent passes grade_data fields to ListClassGrades
Parent passes grade_data fields to AddStudent
Parent passes grade_data fields to AddCourse
